subject: animals
content: Dogs are mammals. Mammals have fur and feed their babies milk. There are many kinds of mammals.

subject: animals
content: Elephants are the biggest land animals. They use their long trunks to drink water and pick up things.

subject: animals
content: Birds have wings and feathers. Most birds can fly. Baby birds hatch from eggs.

subject: animals
content: Penguins are birds that cannot fly. They swim very fast in the cold ocean. They live in groups called colonies.

subject: animals
content: Fish live in water and breathe through gills. They have scales on their bodies. There are thousands of kinds of fish.

subject: animals
content: Dolphins are very smart animals. They live in the ocean but breathe air. Dolphins talk to each other with clicks and whistles.

subject: animals
content: A habitat is where an animal lives. Some animals live in forests. Some live in the ocean. Some live in deserts.

subject: animals
content: Lions live on the savanna. The savanna has tall grass and warm weather. Lions rest in the shade during the day.

subject: animals
content: Animals that eat only plants are called herbivores. Cows, rabbits, and deer are herbivores. They have flat teeth for chewing plants.

subject: animals
content: Animals that eat other animals are called carnivores. Lions and eagles are carnivores. They have sharp teeth or beaks.

subject: animals
content: Insects have six legs and three body parts. Butterflies, ants, and bees are all insects. Most insects have wings.

subject: animals
content: Bees live in hives and make honey. Bees help flowers grow by moving pollen. A group of bees is called a colony.

subject: animals
content: Reptiles are cold-blooded animals. They warm up by sitting in the sun. Snakes, turtles, and lizards are reptiles.

subject: animals
content: Many baby animals have special names. A baby dog is called a puppy. A baby cat is called a kitten. A baby cow is called a calf.

subject: animals
content: Animals make sounds to talk to each other. Birds sing songs. Frogs make croaking sounds. Wolves howl at the moon.
